chief_complaint: A 30-year-old male patient presented with jaundice, abdominal pain, and gastrointestinal symptoms following the initiation of ofatumumab for multiple sclerosis.
history: The patient had newly diagnosed multiple sclerosis and initially responded well to high-dose corticosteroids. He was started on ofatumumab as disease-modifying therapy. Baseline laboratory investigations, viral screening, and autoantibody testing were normal. He developed symptoms within days of the first injection.
exam: Vital signs: not specified. Physical examination findings: jaundice, abdominal pain, and gastrointestinal symptoms.
labs: Laboratory studies revealed severe hepatocellular injury with hyperbilirubinemia, coagulopathy, elevated pancreatic enzymes, and positive anti-smooth muscle antibodies. Imaging demonstrated gallstone disease, which later progressed to acute calculous cholecystitis and severe necrotising pancreatitis.
correct_diagnosis: Autoimmune hepatitis and necrotising pancreatitis following ofatumumab
differentials: Autoimmune hepatitis and necrotising pancreatitis following ofatumumab
correct_ranking: Autoimmune hepatitis and necrotising pancreatitis following ofatumumab
